invariants.claim: Agreement/MVB semantics live outside NovaSeal core.
Repay terminal path is only before or at expiry.
Claim/default terminal path is only after expiry.
Borrower closes by repayment; lender claims after expiry.
Each implemented terminal path creates a NovaAgreementReceiptV0 output.
Wrong terms_hash output binding is rejected by Agreement Profile scripts.
Wrong receipt_hash output binding is rejected by Agreement Profile scripts.
Native CKB funding, repayment, and claim output shapes are locally verified.
Compiled Agreement Profile action ELFs enforce terminal-path guards in CKB VM.
Action/type scripts and the local harness lock execute as lock/type groups in resolved CKB transactions.
Under-capacity agreement outputs are rejected by the CKB transaction verification stack.
Repayment and default payout outputs are bound by typed NativeCkbPayoutV0 output data.
Each signed Agreement intent commits to the NovaSealCanonicalEnvelopeV0 hash and the runtime recomputes the same hash before accepting.
Value-bearing u64 additions in terminal paths are guarded before principal+fee, nonce+1, and payout capacity floor calculations.
invariants.location: repay_before_expiry, claim_after_expiry, and nova_agreement_lifecycle arithmetic guards

invariants.claim: Agreement borrower/lender authority identifiers are bound to signer pubkeys and payout recipient lock args at the profile boundary.
invariants.location: borrower_sig/lender_sig pubkey equality guards and typed NativeCkbPayoutV0 recipient checks

invariants.claim: Agreement terminal arithmetic rejects u64 overflow at principal+fee and nonce+1 boundaries, with max/max-1 regression fixtures.
invariants.location: repay_before_expiry, claim_after_expiry, nova_agreement_lifecycle, and arithmetic boundary fixtures

invariants.claim: Wallet-facing signing vectors cover originate, repay, and claim with fixed-width signed intent bytes, BIP340 message hashes, signer sets, and displayed canonical envelope hashes.
Originate -> repay and originate -> claim execute as live devnet lifecycles with fresh provenance and negative dry-run rejection evidence.
